MASTER JURY CALENDAR OF OCT-31-2022 -- APPEARANCES BY DOW PATTEN, ESQ. FOR PLAINTIFF DORA BARNES; AMY SUPER, ESQ. AND IAN ELIASOPH, ESQ. FOR DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO -- ASSIGNED TO JURY TRIAL CALENDAR ON OCT-31-2022 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 502, JUDGE JEFFREY S. ROSS. PARTIES WERE ORDERED TO CONTACT DEPT. 502 BY E-MAIL BY TODAY AND THE CLERK'S PHONE NUMBER WAS ALSO PROVIDED. THE COURT ALSO ASSIGNED CASE TO A MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE. PARTIES WERE ORDERED TO CONTACT THE COURT'S ADR COORDINATOR, ELIZABETH KELBER, BY EMAIL BY 4:00 PM ON 11/2/22 FOR SCHEDULING OF THE MSC. THE ADR COORDINATOR'S PHONE NUMBER WAS ALSO PROVIDED. SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $250.00, PAYABLE BY NOV-17-2022, IS IMPOSED ON ATTY. DOW W. PATTTEN FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH THE COURT'S ORDER PURSUANT TO THE NOTICE OF TIME AND PLACE OF TRIAL AND TRIAL ORDER. ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE IS SET ON NOV-18-2022 AT 9:30AM IN DEPT. 206. OSC WILL BE DISCHARGED IF SANCTIONS ARE PAID PRIOR TO THE HEARING DATE. TRIAL TIME ESTIMATE: 14 DAYS. JUDGE: SAMUEL K. FENG; CLERK: JIROQUE; COURT REPORTER: MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Sep-30-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Dow W. Patten, Esq., FORTHRIGHT LAW, P.C., (415) 228-6848 for Plaintiff Dora Barnes,
Amy Super, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, (415) 554-3931, amy.super@sfcityatty.org, for Defendant CCSF.
Connie Parchman CSR#6137, Jan Brown & Associates, 510-853-5260, parchman1@gmail.com, Court reporter,

ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication

Defendant City and County of San Francisco's ("CCSF") motion for summary judgment or alternatively, summary adjudication is denied.

CCSF's separate statement of undisputed facts does not comply with CRC 3.1350. First, defendant fails to list the evidence supporting the purported undisputed facts in the correct column. (See CRC 3.1350(h).) Second, the alternative motion for summary adjudication does not comply with CRC 3.1350(b)["If made in the alternative, a motion for summary adjudication may make reference to and depend on the same evidence submitted in support of the summary judgment motion. If summary adjudication is sought, whether separately or as an alternative to the motion for summary judgment, the specific cause of action, affirmative defense, claims for damages, or issues of duty must be stated specifically in the notice of motion and be repeated, verbatim, in the separate statement of undisputed material facts."]

In addition, defendant fails to maintain its burden of production per CCP 437(p)(2). The limited facts that defendant cites fail to show that plaintiff cannot establish her claims. For example, defendant argues that plaintiff cannot show adverse employment actions. But CCSF's own evidence refers to a 10-day and 30-day suspension. (UMF 15, 19.) CCSF additionally argues that it had legitimate reasons for the adverse actions taken against plaintiff. CCSF, however, fails to show that the decisionmakers based their decisions on legitimate criteria. Mr. Hsuing notes that plaintiff was twice suspended, but he does not declare that he made those decisions. In fact, the evidence that CCSF proffers shows that other individuals (Kauffman and Rohrer) issued the suspensions. (Hsuing Decl., Exs. D, F.) There is no evidence from those decisionmakers showing that their decisions were based on good faith reasons and that they lacked animus.

In sum, it may very well be that defendant had good cause to discipline plaintiff, but defendant's mash-up of facts is insufficient to carry its summary judgment/adjudication burden.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling order in compliance with CRC3.1312.

Judge: Vedica Puri, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Connie Parchman CSR#6137, Jan Brown & Associates, 510-853-5260, parchman1@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)
